article_meta.title: New York Playbook POLITICO's must-read briefing informing the daily conversation among knowledgeable New Yorkers New Yor

reasoning: The model repeatedly flagged quoted/attributed source statements (including Q&A responses and direct quotes) as author bias, with only one valid author-prose instance identified. This shows a systematic attribution rule failure and several false positives, while missing at least one other plausible author-level bias instance.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Four of five flagged items are from clearly attributed source language (Sen. Gounardes’ Q&A responses and a direct quote from John Kaehny), violating the attribution rule.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Most flagged items are not author prose; only 'murky ethics' is a legitimate author-level bias instance.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model missed other defensible author-prose bias such as the loaded phrasing 'evading the prohibition against lobbyist donating.'
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: For the single valid item ('murky ethics'), the label 'Subjective Qualifying Adjectives' is appropriate; otherwise type accuracy cannot redeem the broader attribution errors.
